 def parse_host_port(value: str, default_port: int = 80) -> tuple[str, int]:
+    """Parse a host:port string and return a (host, port) tuple.
+
+    Supports plain hostnames, host:port pairs, and IPv6 addresses enclosed
+    in brackets, e.g. "[::1]:8080". If the port is omitted, ``default_port``
+    is returned.
+    """
+    # Handle IPv6 literal with brackets
+    if value.startswith("["):
+        try:
+            close = value.index("]")
+        except ValueError:
+            # Malformed bracket handling â€“ fall back to the old logic
+            host, sep, port_text = value.partition(":")
+            if not sep:
+                return value, default_port
+            if port_text == "":
+                return host, default_port
+            return host, int(port_text)
+        host = value[1:close]
+        rest = value[close+1:]
+        if rest.startswith(":"):
+            port_text = rest[1:]
+            if port_text == "":
+                return host, default_port
+            return host, int(port_text)
+        else:
+            return host, default_port
+    # Non-bracketed case
     host, sep, port_text = value.partition(":")
     if not sep:
         return value, default_port
"
